title: Add Contact page with WhatsApp and email links

requirements.text: Create a new Contact page at route /contact in the frontend. The page must display the phone number 9137202881 as a clickable WhatsApp link (https://wa.me/919137202881) and the email address ashokgold664@gmail.com as a clickable mailto link (mailto:ashokgold664@gmail.com). Both links must be prominently styled using the site's existing bright gold-accent theme (gold borders, gold text on hover, cream/ivory background). The page should include clear labels (e.g., 'WhatsApp Us' and 'Email Us') with appropriate icons or visual indicators to differentiate the two contact methods.

quotes: give a page with a contact and email 9137202881 ashokgold664@gmail.com link them to WhatsApp and email

acceptanceCriteria: A /contact route is registered in App.tsx and renders the Contact page.
The phone number 9137202881 is displayed and clicking it opens WhatsApp via https://wa.me/919137202881 in a new tab.
The email ashokgold664@gmail.com is displayed and clicking it opens the default email client via mailto:ashokgold664@gmail.com.
The page uses the site's bright cream/ivory background and gold accent colors consistent with the existing theme.
A 'Contact' link is added to the Navigation bar (desktop and mobile menus) pointing to /contact.
A 'Contact' link is added to the Footer's quick navigation links pointing to /contact.

constraints: Do not modify any files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
Do not change any backend logic — this is a frontend-only addition.
WhatsApp link must use the international format with country code 91 (India): https://wa.me/919137202881.
nonGoals: No contact form or message submission functionality.
No backend storage of contact inquiries.
No third-party integrations beyond the WhatsApp deep link and mailto protocol.
